description: Figure 14 displays a 3D stacked bar chart of the relative cation fraction (at.%) of different metal valence states in the oxide films of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs), derived from XPS peak-area analysis. The x-axis shows aggregated elemental reference columns for Al, Fe, and Mn indicating overall valence contributions, followed by individual sample columns: S0 (as-hot-rolled), S950, S1050, and S1150 (solution-treated at 950–1150 °C). The legend identifies Al³⁺, Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺_ox, Fe³⁺_hy, Mn⁴⁺, and Mn²⁺. Al³⁺ is the predominant species across all samples. The fine-grained S950 sample exhibits the highest Al³⁺ fraction at 90.17 at.%, while the as-rolled S0 shows substantially higher Fe and Mn contents alongside reduced Al. As grain coarsening progresses from S950 to S1150, the Al³⁺ content progressively declines and Fe/Mn contents rise; the caption states that Al in solution-treated samples nonetheless remains higher than in S0. Fe is distributed as Fe²⁺, Fe³⁺_ox, and Fe³⁺_hy, whereas Mn presents as Mn²⁺ and Mn⁴⁺. These trends confirm that grain refinement drives preferential Al dissolution and dense Al-rich oxide film formation, enhancing corrosion resistance, whereas coarse-grained structures yield thinner, less Al-enriched films.
